name: Sacred Dance (Kagura)
shortDescription: Kagura is a type of Shinto ritual dance performed to entertain the gods and evoke their presence, often involving music and elaborate costumes.
description:
Kagura is a central element within Shinto ritual practices, representing a sacred dance performed to entertain and appease the kami (spirits or gods). Its origins are deeply intertwined with the myth of Amaterasu, the sun goddess, who retreated into a cave, plunging the world into darkness. To lure her out, the other deities performed a raucous and comedic dance, led by Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, the goddess of dawn and revelry. This dance, considered the prototype of Kagura, successfully enticed Amaterasu to emerge, restoring light to the world. 

Kagura encompasses a wide range of performance styles, varying by region and shrine. It can range from highly formalized and solemn rituals performed by miko (shrine maidens) to more theatrical and entertaining performances involving masked dancers and elaborate costumes. The dances often depict scenes from mythology, local legends, or agricultural practices, serving as a form of storytelling and communal expression. Music, typically consisting of traditional instruments like flutes, taiko drums, and stringed instruments, plays a vital role in creating the atmosphere and guiding the movements of the dancers. The purpose of Kagura extends beyond mere entertainment; it is believed to purify the space, invoke the presence of the kami, and ensure good fortune for the community. Different forms of Kagura include Mikomai (performed by shrine maidens), Izumo-ryū Kagura (associated with Izumo Grand Shrine), and Ise-ryū Kagura (associated with Ise Grand Shrine), each with its own unique characteristics and traditions. Modern Kagura performances continue to be an important part of Shinto festivals and cultural events, preserving ancient traditions and connecting people to their spiritual heritage.

sources.title: Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697
sources.author: W.G. Aston
